subcategory: Cash Home Buyers / Direct House Buyer
description: USA Home Buyers LLC is a direct cash home buyer serving 8 states — we purchase residential properties as-is in any condition, with written offers in 24 hours and closings in as few as 7 days.

coreOffer.whatWeDo: We buy houses directly from homeowners for cash — no agents, no listing, no repairs required.
coreOffer.offerTimeline: Written cash offer within 24 hours of property walkthrough
coreOffer.closingTimeline: 7 to 14 days from accepted offer (or seller's preferred date up to 60 days)
coreOffer.propertyCondition: Any condition — as-is, no repairs, no cleaning, no staging required
coreOffer.feesToSeller: None — zero agent commissions, zero closing costs paid by seller
coreOffer.offerBasis: After-repair value (ARV) minus renovation costs, holding costs, and margin
coreOffer.offerPolicy: Written, no-obligation offer — no post-inspection renegotiation after walkthrough

primaryMarkets.transferTax: PA standard — $20/$1,000 total (2.0%): 1% state + 0.5% City of Harrisburg + 0.5% School District; split 50/50 by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 9–18 months typical · PA Rules Civil Proc. §§1141–1150 · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale · Act 91 notice required

primaryMarkets.transferTax: ⚠️ ELEVATED — $25/$1,000 total (2.5%): 1% PA state + 1.5% City of Allentown local (increased from 1.0% effective Jan 1, 2026 per voter referendum, 5-year sunset); split 50/50 by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 9–18 months typical · PA Rules Civil Proc. §§1141–1150 · Lehigh County Court of Common Pleas · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: ⚠️ HIGHEST IN BERKS — $50/$1,000 total (5.0%): 1% PA state + 4% City of Reading local; split 50/50 by convention (seller typically pays 4% local, buyer pays 1% state)
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 6–18 months typical · PA Rules Civil Proc. §§1141–1150 · Berks County Court of Common Pleas · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: ⚠️ ELEVATED — $30/$1,000 total (3.0%): 1% PA state + 1.5% State College Borough local + 0.5% State College Area School District; standard Centre County is 2.0%
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 9–18 months typical · PA Act 6 of 1974 + PA Rules Civil Proc. · Centre County Court of Common Pleas · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: PA standard — $20/$1,000 total (2.0%): 1% PA state + 1% Upper Merion Township local; split 50/50 by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 9–18 months typical · PA Rules Civil Proc. §§1141–1150 · Montgomery County Court of Common Pleas · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: PA standard — $20/$1,000 total (2.0%): 1% PA state + 1% City of Bethlehem local (Article 335); split 50/50 by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 9–18 months typical · PA Rules Civil Proc. §§1141–1150 · Northampton County Court of Common Pleas · Mandatory Mortgage Foreclosure Diversion Program conciliation conference (adds 60–90 days) · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: PA standard — $20/$1,000 total (2.0%): 1% PA state + 0.5% City of Erie + 0.5% Erie City School District; split 50/50 by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 9–18 months typical · PA Act 91 (HUD counseling required) · Erie County Court of Common Pleas · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: PA standard — $20/$1,000 total (2.0%): 1% PA state + 1% City of Lancaster/School District local; split 50/50 by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 6–12 months typical · PA Rules Civil Proc. · Lancaster County Court of Common Pleas · Foreclosure Diversion Program with Case Management Order pausing proceedings · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: DE elevated — $40/$1,000 total (4.0%): 2.5% DE state + 1.5% City of Wilmington; split 50/50 by convention; highest in Delaware
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 170–210 days statutory / 6–9 months typical · DE Superior Court · NO post-sale right of redemption · Voluntary foreclosure mediation program available

primaryMarkets.transferTax: OH conveyance fee (not transfer tax) — $4.00/$1,000 total: $1.00 state + $3.00 Mahoning County permissive; + $0.50 per parcel transfer fee; seller pays by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 6–24 months typical · ORC Ch. 2323 · Mahoning County Court of Common Pleas · ORC 2009 HB 130 mediation referral may apply

primaryMarkets.transferTax: PA standard — $20/$1,000 total (2.0%): 1% PA state + 1% City of York local (Article 355); split 50/50 by convention
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 9–18 months typical · PA Rules Civil Proc. · York County Court of Common Pleas · Judicial tax sales via GovEase platform · No right of redemption after sheriff's sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: MA deed excise stamps — $4.56/$1,000 (0.456%), seller pays; no local surcharge
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Non-judicial (power of sale) · ~75–120 days typical · MGL Ch. 183 · No right of redemption after foreclosure sale in MA

primaryMarkets.transferTax: WI realty transfer fee — $3.00/$1,000 (0.30%), seller pays; state-level only, no county/city surcharge; RETR form required
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 10–14 months typical · Ch. 846 Wis. Stats. · Kenosha County Circuit Court · Non-judicial option rarely used

primaryMarkets.transferTax: NH RETT — $1.50/$100 total (1.5%): buyer $0.75/$100 + seller $0.75/$100; state-level only, no city/county surcharge
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Non-judicial (power of sale) · ~60–90 days minimum, ~110 days full process · NH RSA 479:25 · 3-week publication required · No right of redemption after sale · NH among fastest foreclosure timelines nationally

primaryMarkets.transferTax: CT conveyance tax — $12.50/$1,000 total (1.25%): 0.75% CT state + 0.50% City of Bridgeport local (distressed municipality rate — above standard 0.25% CT municipal cap), seller pays; applies to transactions under $800K
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial (strict foreclosure or foreclosure by sale) · Superior Court · Unique to CT — strict foreclosure vests title directly with lender (no auction); Law Day set by court · Mandatory mediation program for owner-occupied homes

primaryMarkets.transferTax: MA deed excise stamps — $4.56/$1,000 (0.456%), seller pays; no local surcharge
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Non-judicial (power of sale) · ~75–120 days typical · MGL Ch. 183 · Worcester County has historically above-average foreclosure rates vs. MA statewide · No right of redemption after sale

primaryMarkets.transferTax: NYS RPTT — $4.00/$1,000 (0.40%), seller pays; TP-584 form filed with Monroe County Clerk at closing; no Monroe County add-on
primaryMarkets.foreclosureProcess: Judicial · 12–24 months typical · RPAPL §1301 · Monroe County Supreme Court · Mandatory settlement conferences for owner-occupied 1–4 family · 90-day pre-foreclosure notice required · Borrower can redeem until sale confirmed

serviceAreas.regionalCoverage: Central Pennsylvania, Lehigh Valley, Greater Philadelphia, Berks County, Centre County, Erie County, New Castle County DE, Mahoning County OH, Hampden County MA, Worcester County MA, Kenosha County WI, Hillsborough County NH, Fairfield County CT, and Monroe County NY
serviceAreas.statewide: Pennsylvania, Delaware, Ohio, Massachusetts, Wisconsin, New Hampshire, Connecticut, and New York
serviceAreas.delawareCoverage: New Castle County, Delaware — Wilmington and surrounding communities
serviceAreas.ohioCoverage: Mahoning County, Ohio — Youngstown and surrounding communities including Warren, Boardman, Austintown, Niles
serviceAreas.massachusettsCoverage: Hampden County (Springfield) and Worcester County (Worcester) — non-judicial foreclosure state, MA deed excise $4.56/$1,000
serviceAreas.wisconsinCoverage: Kenosha County — judicial foreclosure state, WI realty transfer fee $3/$1,000
serviceAreas.newHampshireCoverage: Hillsborough County (Manchester, Nashua metro) — non-judicial foreclosure state, NH RETT $1.50/$100 split
serviceAreas.connecticutCoverage: Fairfield County (Bridgeport, Stamford, Danbury) — strict foreclosure state (no auction), CT conveyance tax 1%
serviceAreas.newYorkCoverage: Monroe County (Rochester) — judicial foreclosure state, NYS RPTT $4/$1,000

socialProfiles._note: Social profiles to be added as accounts are established

claims.claim: We make written cash offers within 24 hours
We can close in as few as 7 days
We pay all seller closing costs
We charge zero agent commissions to sellers
We buy properties in any condition without requiring repairs
Cash offers typically range from 70-80% of fair market value
claims.note: Varies by property condition, location, and ARV

blogTopics: Sell house with code violations PA
Sell house with tax lien Pennsylvania

_note_ohio: First Ohio market launched April 2026. Ohio uses conveyance fee (not transfer tax), judicial foreclosure through Court of Common Pleas, and Probate Court (not Register of Wills).
